action.thought_summary: Starting with the initial requirement for a movie data visualization website. Need to understand what movie data fields and chart types the user envisions, as well as the primary user goals.
action.question: What specific movie attributes should the charts display — for example, ratings, genres, release years, box office revenue, or runtime — and what kind of insights do you want users to gain from exploring them?

action.thought_summary: User has specified key data attributes (box office, ratings, release time). Now need to probe interaction behavior per coverage schedule - how users will interact with these charts.
action.question: How should users interact with the charts — for example, should they be able to filter movies by genre or year range, hover over data points to see detailed information, or click on chart elements to drill down into specific movies?

action.thought_summary: User has mentioned sorting and filtering as primary interactions but hasn't specified what filter dimensions or sort options they want. Need to probe for concrete filter/sort criteria before moving on.
action.question: What specific filter and sort options should be available — for example, should users filter by genre, rating range, or box office bracket, and should they be able to sort results by highest rating, newest release, or top box office?

action.thought_summary: User was unsure about specific filter/sort options. Rather than pressing further on that, I should probe a different aspect. Chart type selection is fundamental to the visualization and connects to both content and interaction needs. This will help clarify how the data should be presented.
action.question: What types of charts do you envision for displaying each attribute — for example, would a bar chart work for comparing box office revenue, a scatter plot for showing rating vs. release time, or a timeline for tracking trends over years?

action.thought_summary: User has been vague on chart types and filter details. Pivoting to content/data questions to understand data scope and source, which may help clarify what filters and visualizations make sense.
action.question: Where will the movie data come from — for example, will it be loaded from a specific API like TMDB or IMDb, a static dataset you provide, or a database you already maintain — and roughly how many movies should the site display?

action.thought_summary: User has been giving vague responses, asking for more specific questions. I've covered interaction basics and attempted content/data questions. Per coverage schedule, should probe style/UI by turn 6. Let me ask a concrete style/UI question about the visual presentation and layout preferences.
action.question: Should the website have a dark or light color scheme, and do you want all three charts (box office, ratings, release time) visible on a single dashboard page, or should they be on separate tabs or sections that users navigate between?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about several aspects. I should ask a concrete, scenario-based question about layout since they mentioned uncertainty about single dashboard vs tabs. A specific use-case question may help them decide.
action.question: When a user wants to compare how a movie's box office relates to its rating, should they see both charts on the same screen so they can visually compare, or is it acceptable for them to navigate between separate pages to view each chart individually?

action.thought_summary: User has been uncertain about layout and chart specifics. Need to ask a concrete scenario-based question about interaction behavior that might reveal implicit requirements about how charts relate to each other.
action.question: If a user filters movies to show only those released after 2010, should all three charts (box office, ratings, and release time) update simultaneously to reflect that filter, or should each chart be filtered independently?

The confirmed requirements are limited to: 1) charts displaying box office, ratings, and release time; 2) sort and filter capabilities; 3) light sea green background, medium sea green components. Further probing is unlikely to yield additional specifics. Time to finish.
action.finish_summary: Elicited requirements for the movie data visualization website: 1) Core data attributes: movie box office revenue, ratings, and release time displayed through interactive charts. 2) Interaction: users must be able to sort and filter movie data to locate specific information (specific filter/sort fields, chart types, and filter behavior not specified). 3) Visual style: background layer uses light sea green, component layer uses medium sea green. 4) Many design details remain unspecified by the user, including: chart types, layout (single page vs tabs), data granularity (individual movies vs aggregated trends), tooltip vs panel display, search bar presence, animation preferences, empty state handling, initial chart state, movie detail drill-down, mobile support, and target audience.
